context: =  NEUROENDOCRINE TUMOR OF GI TRACT [Karzinoide, German = carcinoma-like] =  group of well-differentiated tumors of the diffuse endocrine system outside pancreas + thyroid Commonality:   neuroendocrine cells share several antigens with nerve elements (neuroendocrine markers) Neuroendocrine system: ◊  The majority of neuroendocrine tumors occur within the gastroenteropancreatic (GEP) axis (a)  adrenal medulla, pancreatic islets, paraganglia (b)  parathyroid, pituitary, thyroid C cells (c)  others: ›  GI tract: from hypopharynx to rectum (67-85%) ›  tracheobronchial system (10-29%) ›  biliary tr
